one_time_description: Special partnership distribution from Nodin Kitagan Limited Partnerships (Bow Lake Wind Farm) received April 3, 2019, used partly to repay Siemens Financial Ltd. debt of $16,525,823 plus fees of $3,077,606
economic_notes: First Nation operates wind energy (Bow Lake Wind Farm 60MW, Goulais Wind Farm 25MW), solar energy projects (rooftop and ground mount), industrial park through BBI, bingo operations, and various band-operated enterprises. COVID-19 declared pandemic March 11, 2020 with potential future financial impacts noted.
notes: Large one-time item: $25.5M special distribution from Nodin Kitagan Limited Partnerships (wind farm) received April 3, 2019 drove significant surplus of $13.3M vs prior year $2.6M. This also enabled repayment of $16.5M Siemens Financial Ltd. debt reducing long-term debt from $36.5M to $18.4M. Seven GBEs accounted for on modified equity basis including wind and solar energy partnerships. Segmented information note appears truncated in source document. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $11,856,934 + Province of Ontario $2,424,257 + Government of Canada $782,514 + North Shore Tribal Council $1,381,717 + Government of Canada Trust Funds $27,673 = $16,473,095; Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership ($1,499,030) included as government transfer given Robinson Huron Treaty distribution nature, bringing total to $17,490,348. OSR includes band enterprises, bingo, renewable energy and other revenues. Annual debt service reflects principal payments on long-term debt per cash flow statement.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
